page: Amazon Web Services c8i.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server instance featuring the x86_64 architecture. It is powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors operating at 3.9 GHz, delivering 96 physical cores and 192 vCPUs via hyperthreading. The hardware configuration includes 384.0 GB of system memory, which translates to a resource density of 2.0 GB per physical core. Cache architecture consists of 48 KB L1d, 2 MB L2, and a large 480 MB L3 cache. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, but features a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier (top 10%) for multi-core CPU performance, as demonstrated by stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core metrics. Memory bandwidth is also highly rated, with both small-block cached reads and large-block uncached reads and writes scoring in the top 10%. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE and AMX_INT8), which contribute to top-tier performance in extended instructions, encryption, and physics simulations. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core results falling in the middle 50%, though Geekbench single-core scores remain in the top 10%.

This server is designed for highly parallelized, compute-intensive workloads. It achieves top-tier throughput in software compilation, ray tracing, image processing, and static web serving. For database operations, it demonstrates top-tier efficiency, supported by high Redis SET operations throughput. It also shows strong to top-tier performance in LLM inference prompt processing and text generation across small, medium, and large models. However, the lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, and the 2.0 GB of RAM per core represents a tradeoff for memory-bound applications.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.metal-48xl is an x86_64 bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors running at 3.9 GHz. It features 96 physical cores, 192 vCPUs, 384.0 GB of RAM, and a 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, with no local storage or GPUs. This instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, supported by advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. While single-core performance is moderate, its massive parallel processing capabilities yield top-tier results in software compilation, ray tracing, and LLM inference. It is suited for high-throughput compute workloads, static web serving, and parallelized database tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.metal-48xl is a compute-optimized bare-metal server with 192 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.

tagline: A bare-metal, compute-optimized platform featuring 192 vCPUs and advanced matrix extensions for high-throughput parallel processing and database workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores.
384.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per physical core.
Top-tier multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations.
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and Intel AMX for matrix operations.
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU accelerators.
Suited for software compilation, ray tracing, static web serving, and LLM inference.
